Original:
Navigation Directions in this complex space.
 - To The Healer: Always downward
 - To Nowhere: Everything else

Welcome to The Healer’s lair—made possible by
your questionable decisions and poor life choices.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Ah, so you’re the newcomer... Aiden.
You’re later than I expected. I’ve been 
waiting for you for many years.

Name: Aiden
Original:
Waiting for me? But I don’t even know you...
And where am I, anyway? This house just 
appeared out of nowhere!

Name: The Healer
Original:
You’re in the Oblivium, a place that exists 
between what was and what is yet to be.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Are you alright, child? I’ve been calling you,
but you didn’t respond.

Name: Aiden
Original: You... I’ve seen you before... Ahh, my head...

Name: The Healer
Original: Migraines? Let me take a look.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Music... It’s curious, isn’t it? It can help 
find balance, align body and mind.

Name: The Healer
Original:
You seem calmer now. Tell me, Aiden, 
can you recall what day it is?

Name: The Healer
Original:
I see... Near-total amnesia. Perhaps it’s
not such a bad thing...

Name: The Healer
Original:
The Oblivium is a strange place...
And a generous one. It will allow you to 
reclaim your memories, one by one.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Here, everything changes based on your feelings.
But be careful. What you recover isn’t just an
image of the past, it’s a reflection of yourself.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Yes, and sometimes that reflection can
be hard to face. Your mind, be it light or
shadow, will guide you...or lead you astray.

Name: The Healer
Original:
Here, take this journal. It will help
you track your state of mind and the memories
you recover.

Name: The Healer
Original:
One day, you’ll have to make a final 
choice. But for now, it’s too soon to decide...

Name: The Healer
Original:
Keep the journal close. One day, 
it will reveal what you seek.

\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[50,50]Am I dead?\| Or... maybe dreaming?\| 
I can’t feel anything...

Name: The Host
Original:
Welcome to The Oblivium. As its ruler, I formally 
greet you.

Name: The Host
Original:
Now, more importantly... What do you think 
of the decor? I did it myself!

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[50,50](a strange voice)
Oh, but by 'myself,' I mean...\| well, isn't that 
something for you to figure out? Haha!

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe! \|Catchy, right? ...Well, you’ll get it eventually.
For now, shouldn’t you be asking something more important?
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[50,50](Who am I...?\| Why can't I remember anything?)

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[50,50](a strange voice) 
Haha! The kid's funny, trying to remember!

Name: The Host
Original:
Let me give you a little advice, Aiden. 
*Here, nothing moves... unless you truly try.*

Name: The Host
Original:
Help the people in the village. Maybe 
your memories will return.

Name: Aiden
Original: The village? But there's nothing here!

Name: The Host
Original:
Oh, one more thing. *Be careful with your emotions. 
Things might get harder otherwise.*

Navigation Directions in this complex space.
- To The Healer: Always downward
- To Almost Nowhere: Almost everything else

Welcome to The Healer’s lair—made possible by 
your questionable decisions and poor life choices.

It is said that long ago, an old man walked so often 
toward the village that his steps became a path for 
all to follow.

Name: Elderly Man
Original: You startled me!\| I was lost in thought...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
I tried to find my way to my son's village,
but the path keeps hiding from me.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
I can see in your eyes the same look as my 
son...\| Always searching, yet never truly 
finding.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
Perhaps if you help me find my way,
it might guide you as well.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
But I don’t know where to begin... Maybe
there’s something in my house or outside
that could help.

Name: Aiden
Original: This feels off... I guess I should start over.

A park with a relaxing musical zone. 
Step to play chords: Top-Cm, Right-Gm, Left-Dm

You step on the musical square, but nothing plays. 
It seems you lack the inspiration to create music.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
This fear...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
This sadness...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
This anger...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
Now I remember... the direction to the village! 
You must cross a long forest to reach the bridge!

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
Maybe there’s something in my house —or 
outside—that could help finding the path
to the village.

Name: Aiden
Original:
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]It's best not to venture out if I don't know
my way around.

On the bedside table rests an old vinyl player.
Would you like to play a record?

Composed in fear, by an unknown artist, with chords: 
Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Cm-Dm.

Name: Aiden
Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Maybe I could go check out the park now...

Composed in sorrow, by an unknown artist, with chords:
Cm-Gm-Dm-Dm-Gm-Cm.

Composed in anger, by an unknown artist, with chords:
Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Dm-Cm.

The chosen vinyl conveys fear, with chords:
 Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Cm-Dm
Would you like to play it again?

The chosen vinyl conveys sorrow, with chords:
 Cm-Gm-Dm-Dm-Gm-Cm
Would you like to play it again?

The chosen vinyl conveys anger, with chords:
 Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Dm-Cm
Would you like to play it again?
\pop[7] Well boy, you should try harder.
\pop[8] Is he even worth watching? What a waste of time.

Name: Aiden
Original:
Ugh, the bridge seems to be broken. What should 
I do?\| *I have a feeling this could affect my 
mind or my memories.*
Don’t do anything and go find the old man.

Ugh, I can't seem to be able to repair
the bridge...I better go find the old man.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
You really did fix the bridge. Good work, 
now let’s keep going, we’re nearly there.

Name: Aiden
Original: I’d rather not go through the forest now.
Cross the bridge to reach the village.

The village is just ahead. 
Thank you, young one...

I hope my son will agree to see me...\| Family...\|
such a strange bond, isn't it? \|The source of 
the greatest joys, and the deepest sorrows.

Take this. I hope this token can help you
find what you’re looking for.

Name: Aiden
Original: A memory token?... Wait... My head...

Name: Grandma
Original:
Aiden, what are you doing daydreaming like that? 
Come on, I’ll push you on the swing!

Name: Grandma
Original:
*This child has already been through far 
too much for his age...*

Name: Little Aiden
Original: Look, Grandma! I'm touching the sky!

Name: Grandma
Original:
Careful, little bird! 
Don’t fly too far from me!

Name: Grandma
Original: *I had to take him away from his own mother...*

Name: Grandma
Original: Hm?\| Ah... yes, you are, sweetheart.

Name: Aiden
Original:
This house feels familiar... maybe I'll find something that 
might help me remember.

Name: Grandma
Original: Look up, Aiden. The sky is really beautiful, isn’t it?

Name: Little Aiden
Original:
Wow!\| Do you see that Grandma...
The stars, they shine like little lamps!

Name: Grandma
Original:
Yes, I see it. And look, if you trace lines 
between the stars, you can draw in the sky.

Name: Little Aiden
Original:
Grandma, look!\| If I connect the one on the 
left with the one on the right and back to 
the left, it looks like a chicken!

Name: Grandma
Original:
Yes, I see your chicken. Do you want us to
draw some friends for it?

Name: Little Aiden
Original:
Hmm... I’m not sure. It’s next to that 
lonely star over there that looks like Mom.
Do you think they can be friends?

Name: Grandma
Original:
Yes, my heart, they can be the best friends in
the world.

Name: Little Aiden
Original: Do you think a star could fall into my pocket?

Name: Grandma
Original:
Maybe so... But if a star falls into your 
pocket, you’ll have to take care of it
like a treasure.

Name: Little Aiden
Original:
Say Grandma, if the stars are treasures,
when people die do they join the stars?

Name: Grandma
Original:
Oh, my heart... Maybe so. But we are all 
here with you, and for a very long time.
So don’t you worry.

Name: Little Aiden
Original:
Alright...\| But if ever...\| I’ll look for 
her in the sky.

Name: The Developer
Original:
Thank you for playing this demo! Your feedback 
will help decide if the story continues: [LINK]

Carotopia

Carotopia

Carotopia

Name: The Healer
Original:
You’re in the Oblivium, a place that exists 
between what was and what’s yet to come.

Name: Aiden
Original:
Now’s really not the time to crash in someone 
else’s bed!\|...Weirdly comfy, though.

Name: Aiden
Original:
It’s a big wicker wardrobe. I can’t look
through while she’s watching me.

Name: Aiden
Original:
That’s where she kept the journal.
Now that I look at it more closely... 
this wardrobe feels familiar.

Name: Aiden
Original: A painting of a house with a swing and a pond.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
You scared me!\| 
I was just... lost in thought.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
I have been trying to get to my 
son’s village, but the path keeps 
disappearing on me.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
I can see it in your eyes...\| the same 
look he used to have.\| Always searching 
yet never truly finding.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
If you help me find my way, 
you may find yours too.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
This fear...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... \|I haven’t spoken
to him since...

Name: Aiden
Original:
I should look around. Maybe I’ll find 
something in one of the rooms...

Name: Aiden
Original:
An old photo album.\|
Maybe I...\| Never mind. Let’s open it.

Name: Aiden
Original:
I’ve been here before... haven’t I? 
Maybe something here can help me remember.

Name: Aiden
Original:
I don’t remember much about these pictures... 
but it hurts to look away.

Name: Aiden
Original: Feels nice to be in bed... though it’s a bit small.

Name: Aiden
Original:
This bathtub’s been used so many times...
it’s probably older than me.

Name: Aiden
Original:
It smells like vegetable soup. 
It’s kind of comforting.

Name: Aiden
Original:
I pestered my grandma so much she 
got me my own radio, that, I remember.

Name: Aiden
Original:
There are so many history and geography books... 
They loved to learn about the world.

Name: Aiden
Original: This mirror makes me look older.
That little boy... is that me?
It’s quiet out here... maybe I should start humming.
Hmm... hmm-mmh... hmm... Yeah, that’s terrible.
Haha... it's all starting to look the same out here.
...Is this the right path? I can't see where it ends.
Still... if I go back now, will it be alright?
But what if there’s nothing ahead?
No signs. No guide. Just the weight of my thoughts.
At what point does doubt become insight?

Name: Aiden
Original:
A painting of a lovely house with a 
swing and a pond.

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe! \|Catchy, right? I came up with the name,
had ages to think about it.

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe... well, you’ll get it eventually. For now, 
shouldn’t you be asking something more important?

\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50]Am I dead?\| Or... maybe dreaming?\| 
I can’t feel anything...

Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50](a strange voice)
Oh, but by 'myself,' I mean...\| well, isn't that

\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50](Who am I...?\| Why can't I remember anything?)

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50](a strange voice) 
Haha! The kid's funny, trying to remember!

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe! \|Catchy, right? I came up with the name myself,
had ages to think about it.

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe! \|Catchy, isn’t it? I made the name up myself,
had decade to think about it.

Name: The Host
Original:
Hehe, you'll understand in time. For now, shouldn't 
you be asking something more important?

Name: Aiden
Original:
I just woke up and I’m already back in bed... Guess 
the migraines make me sick. I feel a bit uneasy... resting 
in someone's else's bed... but it's strangely comfortable.

Name: The Host
Original:
Mhm! I made the name up myself, had decade to 
think about it.

Name: The Host
Original:
You'll understand in time. For now, shouldn't 
you be asking something more important?

Name: The Host
Original:
Haha, I made the name up myself, 
had decade to think about it.

\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50]Am I dead?\| Or... \|maybe dreaming?\| 
I can’t feel anything...

Name: The Host
Original:
Haha! You'll understand in time. For now, shouldn't 
you be asking something more important?
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50](Who am I...? Why can't I remember anything?)
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50]Aiden... My name is Aiden, I think...

Name: Aiden
Original: The village? But there's nothing here...

\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50]Am I dead? \|Or is this a dream?
I can't feel anything...
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[100,50]What was that? It felt so familiar...
Haha, it's all starting to look the same out here.
[Wind music effect that comes and dies]
..Is this the right path? I can't see where it ends.
It’s all starting to look the same.
Is this the right path? I can't see where it ends.
No signs, no guide, just the weight of my thoughts.
He said to go straight, they always say that.
Maybe I took a wrong turn...
I can’t see where it ends.
Maybe the old man was mistaken...
or maybe I took a wrong turn.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
I can see it in your eyes...\| the same 
look he used to have. Always searching

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
Maybe if you help me find my way, 
you’ll find yours too.
Is this the right path? I see no end.
If I turn back now, will it be alright?
“Keep going!” What if there’s nowhere to turn?

Name: Aiden
Original:
An old photo album... it feels like 
I should know whose it is.

Original:
I don’t remember these pictures... 
but it hurts to look away.

I hope my son will agree to see me...\\|
Family — such a strange bond, isn't it?
The source of the greatest joys, and

Original:
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Am I dead? Or is this a dream?
I can't feel anything...
en:
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Am I dead? Or is this a dream?
I can't feel anything...
fr:
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Am I dead? Or is this a dream?
I can't feel anything...

Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]What was that? It felt so familiar...
en: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]What was that? It felt so familiar...
fr: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]What was that? It felt so familiar...

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](a strange voice)
Oh, but by 'myself,' I mean...\| well, isn't that 
something for you to figure out? Haha!
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](Who am I...? Why can't I remember anything?)
en: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](Who am I...? Why can't I remember anything?)
fr: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](Who am I...? Why can't I remember anything?)

Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Aiden... My name is Aiden, I think...
en: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Aiden... My name is Aiden, I think...
fr: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Aiden... My name is Aiden, I think...

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[The-Host2]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](a strange voice) 
Haha! The kid's funny, trying to remember!

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
You fixed this bridge alone? Impressive! 
We’re almost there, I can feel it.

Name: Aiden
Original:
Ugh, the bridge seems to be broken. What should I do?\|
*I have a feeling this could affect my mind 
or my memories.*

Name: Aiden
Original:
Ugh, the bridge seems to be broken.
What should I do?\|
*I have a feeling this could affect
my mind or my memories.*

Name: Aiden
Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]This feels off... I guess I should start over.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Ah, so you’re the newcomer... Aiden.
You’re later than I expected. I’ve been 
waiting for you for many years.

Name: Aiden
Original:
\portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Waiting for me? But I don’t even know you...
And where am I, anyway? This house just 
appeared out of nowhere!

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]You’re in the Oblivium, a place that exists 
between what was and what is yet to be.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Are you alright, child? I’ve been calling you,
but you didn’t respond.

Name: Aiden
Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]You... I’ve seen you before... Ahh, my head...

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Music... It’s curious, isn’t it? It can help 
find balance, align body and mind.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]You seem calmer now. Tell me, Aiden, 
can you recall what day it is?

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]I see... Near-total amnesia. Perhaps it’s
not such a bad thing...

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]The Oblivium is a strange place...
And a generous one. It will allow you to 
reclaim your memories, one by one.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Here, everything changes based on your feelings.
But be careful. What you recover isn’t just an
image of the past, it’s a reflection of yourself.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Yes, and sometimes that reflection can
be hard to face. Your mind, be it light or
shadow, will guide you...or lead you astray.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Here, take this journal. It will help
you track your state of mind and the memories
you recover.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]One day, you’ll have to make a final 
choice. But for now, it’s too soon to decide...

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Keep the journal close. One day, 
it will reveal what you seek.

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Welcome to The Oblivium. As its ruler, I formally 
greet you.

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Now, more importantly... What do you think 
of the decor? I did it myself!

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost2]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](a strange voice)
Oh, but by 'myself,' I mean...\| well, isn't that 
something for you to figure out? Haha!

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Haha! You'll understand in time. For now, shouldn't 
you be asking something more important?

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost2]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20](a strange voice) 
Haha! The kid's funny, trying to remember!

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Let me give you a little advice, Aiden. 
*Here, nothing moves... unless you truly try.*

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Help the people in the village. Maybe 
your memories will return.

Name: The Host
Original:
\portrait[TheHost]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Oh, one more thing. *Be careful with your emotions. 
Things might get harder otherwise.*

Name: Elderly Man
Original: \portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20] You startled me!\| I was lost in thought...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]I tried to find my way to my son's village,
but the path keeps hiding from me.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]I can see in your eyes the same look as 
my son...\| Always searching, yet never truly 
finding.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Perhaps if you help me find my way,
it might guide you as well.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]But I don’t know where to begin... Maybe
there’s something in my house or outside
that could help.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]This fear...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]This sadness...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]This anger...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Now I remember... the direction to the village! 
You must cross a long forest to reach the bridge!

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Maybe there’s something in my house —or 
outside—that could help finding the path
to the village.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
This fear...\\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been years 
I didn't speak to him...

Name: Aiden
Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]I missed a note... I guess I should start over.

Name: Elderly Man
Original:
\portrait[ElderlyMan]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]But I don’t know where to begin... Maybe
there’s something in my house—or outside—
that could help.

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Are you alright, child?
I’ve been calling you,\nbut you didn’t respond.

Name: Aiden
Original: \portrait[Aiden]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]You... I’ve seen you before...Ahh, my head...

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]Music... It’s curious, isn’t it?
It can help find balance, align body and mind.

well, isn't that something for you to figure out? Haha!

Name: The Healer
Original:
\portrait[Grandma]\pscale[1]\poff[60,20]You’re in the Oblivium,
a place that exists between what was and 
what is yet to be.

You step on the musical square, but nothing plays. It seems you lack the 
inspiration to create music.
